I’m verifying the module layout and then I’ll run the check directly to see exactly what remains after any changes.

data.text: The direct deadcode run did not report any `internal/federation` symbols at all, which suggests this worktree may already have the needed reachability change or the initial snapshot is stale. I’m verifying the current git state and then I’ll write the decisions log to make the bead’s outcome explicit.

I’ve got the important signal now: the current tree has no `internal/federation` deadcode hits, and the only federation persistence APIs are the `From` helpers used by the hub path. I’m confirming that the old symbol names are absent, then I’ll write the required decisions log and run the project gates.

I’m writing the decisions log now. The content will make the stale-symbol situation explicit so the bead has a durable audit trail even though the current tree already satisfies the reachability check.
The evidence file is in place. I’m running the two required gates now, then I’ll run the repo pre-commit hook before staging and committing the report.
`go test ./...` is still running; the deadcode filter already came back clean for `internal/federation`. I’m waiting for the full test suite to finish before I stage anything.

`go test ./...` is not green in this worktree, and the failures are outside `internal/federation`. I’m writing the required `no_changes_rationale.txt` with the exact blockers so the bead stays in triage instead of pretending the gate passed.
